title: Dryopteris laeta
taxonomy: Dryopteris laeta is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which comprises a large number of species of ferns. The genus Dryopteris is further classified under the order Polypodiales, which is a group of ferns with two distinct types of fronds. Dryopteris laeta is native to certain regions, and its classification as a species of fern places it within the plant kingdom. This classification is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics.

intro: Dryopteris laeta, also known as the creeping wood fern or New Zealand wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the genus Dryopteris. The leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, boost the immune system, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of D. laeta include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the leaves of D. laeta are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions such as eczema and wounds.
